name: Chris Murphy (via Democrats.org)
subject: Dangerous... Unprecedented...
body:
---
Derek, I won’t mince words — our democracy is under threat. Donald Trump spent his first eight months in office making corrupt deals in Washington and overwhelming the American people with chaos, lies, and illegal action. He’s been slashing Medicaid, destroying the economy, and fueling wars abroad — while handing out tax cuts to his billionaire friends and profiting off the presidency for himself. He wants us to believe that we’re powerless. But that’s the biggest lie of all. Every move Trump has made in office has been wildly unpopular, and he knows it. That’s why he pushed Governor Abbott to redistrict Texas. He’s scared of losing seats in Congress in a fair election, so he’s rewriting the rulebook. It’s a blatant power grab and an anti-democratic attempt to rig the 2026 election cycle. **But history tells us that mass mobilization beats a would-be autocrat.** We have to fight back. And we will — but to mobilize, we need a strong infrastructure. We need organizers, technology, logistics, and support. It’s the DNC’s job to build that infrastructure and allocate resources to the states and candidates that need their support most. **[That’s why I’m asking you to pitch in to support the DNC today. What we’re facing right now isn’t politics as usual — it’s a dangerous and unprecedented overreach. The only way we’ll fight back is by working together to mobilize Democrats across the country, and that’s the type of work your $3 contribution will fund:](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?refcode=em_20250908_4544_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4544_22930333_1qeYNp&akid=4544%2E22930333%2E1qeYNp)**
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250908_4544_fr&refcode2=4544_22930333_1qeYNp&akid=4544%2E22930333%2E1qeYNp)**

Every best wish,
Chris Murphy

name: James Carville
subject: Can I count on you to contribute to Abigail Spanberger's campaign for Governor of Virginia?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93781?t=6&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ) Team, I've seen a lot of tough races in my time, so let me tell you:

_This one is as important as it gets._

The Virginia Governor's race is a bellwether for the entire country. This will be the first major election of Trump's second term, and it will set the stage for Democrats in 2026. _If we don't win big here, we'll be facing a rough road ahead._

[So, before I continue, can I count on you to contribute to Abigail Spanberger's campaign for Governor of Virginia and the Voter Protection Project? We can't win this thing without your help.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93782?t=7&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Abigail Spanberger and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93783?t=8&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93784?t=9&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93785?t=10&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93786?t=11&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93782?t=12&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
Here's what's got me terrified, and I won't mince my words: Republicans are going to flood the state with MILLIONS of dollars through their super PACs, trying to keep the Governor's seat red. This election is for all the marbles – and we need to match their energy, their money, and their commitment.

Remember, we only have one opportunity to flip a Governor's office from red to blue in 2025 and it's in Virginia.

And right now, we're seeing that Democratic Governors are our first line of defense against the reckless policies coming out of the Trump Administration.

_Abigail Spanberger is the leader Virginia needs at this moment._

That's why I'm asking you to step up today. The money that's coming from the other side is going to be massive, and we need to be able to push back.

[Will you contribute right now to help Abigail Spanberger and the Voter Protection Project win this race and deliver a major victory for Democrats? Every dollar you contribute will help to flip Virginia blue and keep us on track to win in 2026.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93782?t=13&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Abigail Spanberger and VPP will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93783?t=14&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93784?t=15&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)

[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93786?t=17&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93782?t=18&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ)
Thank you,
James Carville

(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21121%2E878179%2Et5fADZ).**

subject: Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson’s warning…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill

name: Scott Presler
subject: Voter registration deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Early Vote Action PAC](https://go
body:
We don’t have much time

| | | | |
---
Hey friend, it's Scott. I'll keep this quick.
The voter registration deadline in New Jersey is on October 14th, that is less than 6 weeks away.
My team is on the ground and we’re making some big voter registration gains in New Jersey and Pennsylvania before the elections in November. **_[I’m reaching out to you because we need sponsors to help fund our efforts on the ground](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b8XA/?__vbtrk=MzE3OTY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE3OTY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**
With all of the amazing things President Trump is doing in the White House, Republicans are getting complacent and I’m worried if we don’t turn things around we’re going to lose these elections. I’m doing everything I can to wake people up, and I would be really grateful if you’d sponsor one of my voter registration staff for just an hour or two.
**_[You can confirm your sponsorship here](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b8XA/?__vbtrk=MzE3OTY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE3OTY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**

_Thanks,_

**Scott Presler**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b8XA/?__vbtrk=MzE3OTY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE3OTY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: Here’s the deal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
Not only is my opponent, Jack Ciattarelli, endorsed by Donald Trump – he’s also a faithful advocate for the most disastrous elements of Trump’s agenda, and wants to implement them here in New Jersey.
#
Hey, I know my team and I have been emailing you a lot recently asking for your support. But I wanted to take a second to explain why your support matters in this crucial race.
Not only is my opponent, Jack Ciattarelli, endorsed by Donald Trump – he’s also a faithful advocate for the most disastrous elements of Trump’s agenda, and wants to implement them here in New Jersey. He was one of the _biggest cheerleaders_ for the Republican price hike bill that will throw hundreds of thousands of New Jerseyans off their health care.
And now, Ciattarelli’s being supported by a MAGA super PAC that’s pouring _millions of dollars_ into negative ads that make one false assertion after another about my record and my character.
**[In a race this close and with the stakes this high, we need to have the resources to be able to fight back, and we can only do that with your support. Can you split $10 (or whatever you can) between my campaign and State Democrats Committee so we can stop our opponent and protect New Jersey?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94545?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94546?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=2&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94546?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=3&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94546?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=4&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94546?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=5&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94546?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=6&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94546?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=7&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94545?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=8&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa)
---
But this race isn’t about what we’re fighting against — it’s about what we’re fighting _for._
**My vision for our state is simple: Make life better for New Jerseyans.** I’ll fight to bring health care, housing, child care, and energy costs down, tackle crime and keep our communities safe, and defend our constitutional freedoms from the right-wing extremists who are taking our nation down a destructive path.
Here’s the deal — we’re in an extremely competitive race, but lots of voters still haven’t made up their minds. **[Your support helps us spread our message across New Jersey, so we can build the coalition we need to win this race. That’s why I’m humbly asking you to split a contribution between my campaign and State Democrats Committee — it’ll make a critical difference in helping us protect New Jersey.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94545?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa)**
**[DONATE →](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94545?refcode=em20250908-9391&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa)**
Thank you,
Mikie

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. But, if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.statedemocrats.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails_state-democrats-committee/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.statedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_state-democrats-committee/?t=11&akid=9391%2E614464%2EqfgIpa).

name: Steve Daines
subject: We can’t wait
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana
body:
Paid Democrat activists are already flocking to our cities. Leftist propaganda and disinformation are spreading at an alarming rate online. | Fellow American, I need to be blunt.

Our fundraising for the month of September is off to a slow start, severely hindering our ability to reach voters and counter the spread of the Left’s disinformation campaign.

That’s why I’m forced to ask, _**what are we waiting for?**_

Paid Democrat activists are already flocking to our cities. Leftist propaganda and disinformation are spreading at an alarming rate online. All of it bankrolled by a small army of ultra-liberal megadonors bent on ending our Senate Majority and undoing all of President Trump’s achievements.

We don’t have the luxury to wait around until 2026, because the Democrats sure aren’t.

We need to be running at full speed right now, and we can’t do that if our fundraising numbers continue to lag behind. Because I guarantee you, Democrats aren’t having the same problems.

clean_body: Paid Democrat activists are already flocking to our cities. Leftist propaganda and disinformation are spreading at an alarming rate online. | Fellow American, I need to be blunt. Our fundraising for the month of September is off to a slow start, severely hindering our ability to reach voters and counter the spread of the Left’s disinformation campaign. That’s why I’m forced to ask, what are we waiting for? Paid Democrat activists are already flocking to our cities. Leftist propaganda and disinformation are spreading at an alarming rate online. All of it bankrolled by a small army of ultra-liberal megadonors bent on ending our Senate Majority and undoing all of President Trump’s achievements. We don’t have the luxury to wait around until 2026, because the Democrats sure aren’t. We need to be running at full speed right now, and we can’t do that if our fundraising numbers continue to lag behind. Because I guarantee you, Democrats aren’t having the same problems. For America, Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

| | Hi Peter,
**In less than 15 days,** ballots will be counted and**YOU** play a pivotal role in this movement! We need help in electing the **first Latina from Arizona to Congress** and that is where YOU come in!
In a time when our rights and those of immigrants, women, LGBTQ+ and more are under attack, building community, talking to our neighbors door to door, and on the phones about how we can **do something,** is revolutionary.
This fight starts with us—**talking to voters, turning them out, and showing them what’s possible when we organize**. Join us.
---
| | **Here’s what’s happening this week:****Canvassing Door to Door!** Help us make phone calls, go door-to-door and connect with voters face-to-face. In honor of the beginning of Hispanic Heritage Month, join us this weekend,
---

---](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=ab5a40e88c&e=f7c3700ccb)

---
| [RSVP to Tucson Canvass!](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=d644a15389&e=f7c3700ccb)
---
| | For a smoother experience, please**download MiniVAN ahead of your canvass**. [Here](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=68c687eabe&e=f7c3700ccb) is a guide if you need any help. MiniVAN is the program we use to share your list of doors to knock and where we log responses. **Download links** : MiniVAN for [iPhone](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=8bbff602fd&e=f7c3700ccb) and [Android](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=06f386ff04&e=f7c3700ccb).
---
[|

---
| [RSVP to VIRTUAL PHONE BANK](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=6171bc7ae9&e=f7c3700ccb)

| | **For Our Pueblo For Our People Stop at the****University of Arizona!** Tuesday September 16 @ 4pm Gentle Ben’s, 865 E University Blvd. 4:00 PM- 5:00PM
---
| [RSVP to join!](https://adelitaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4efffbdef3b238a45f56f3bb2&id=844814154f&e=f7c3700ccb)
---
| | Thank you for all the energy you’ve brought so far—your time, your voice, and your presence are making a real difference. As we head into these final days, let’s stay grounded in our shared purpose and continue showing up for one another.
If you have any questions or need anything from our team, we’re here to support you. Reach out anytime at **volunteer@adelitaforcongress.com**
Hasta la victoria, **Naomi De La Rosa Arellano**

There are two clear possibilities here:

**Option 1:** We do nothing and allow MAGA Republicans to gut a critical public service that is critical to democracy.

**Option 2:** We fight back to protect USPS offices, postal workers, and the millions of Americans who rely on the USPS to mail-in vote, receive medication, and pay critical bills. **[(SELECT OPTION 2 NOW>>)](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114865052/568420351/793493266?refcode=DOTL_20250908_EMPET_E2_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRkNDkwODIwLTE0OGQtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WnUhJrUy4HdlZSKb8qpOBc0b_1AK-R3oee8tEdgL5_Q=&emci=d4182187-1689-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=4d490820-148d-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=14887570)**

House Democrats are demanding answers and pushing legislation to protect the USPS from partisan sabotage.

_**But we need a surge of grassroots support to show the American people are watching and ready to fight!**_

# [Trump's arbitrary assassinations were enabled by Democrats](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3NzMxODEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM2NTE4MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1v7nvDKlgK43gd0-VS28EfpWj0qBaB5UVpMWYkmfk3Q)
### Observers reacted to Trump's assassination of suspected drug traffickers presumptively described as terrorists, forgetting how Obama and his enablers cheered analogous drone strikes
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Sep 13
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1Nzc3MzE4MSwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMzY1MTgxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.fuZB541hHuDzxHYtQ4h5hDGT9Tt2dNfo9BYyt9IWDXo&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3NzMxODEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM2NTE4MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1v7nvDKlgK43gd0-VS28EfpWj0qBaB5UVpMWYkmfk3Q&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3NzMxODEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM2NTE4MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1v7nvDKlgK43gd0-VS28EfpWj0qBaB5UVpMWYkmfk3Q)
---
| | [](https://substack..pqsRHz9RyH6PKigCWNDVA-0CpDAI_hchjNencj_xCls?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/trumps-arbitrary-assassinations-were?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Among the Trump administration's various daily [assaults on democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/c41928f9-284e-4c4c-ae29-940d34fe4c39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in America, it can be easy to overlook examples that ultimately become inflection points. A close look at what might seem like one-among-many, however, reveals further depths of the emerging dystopia.
Many have wondered what will happen when Trump finally [joins Henry Kissinger in hell](https://substack.com/redirect/e3d1e141-4314-4ba4-afd5-4e397f7b33f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Will the GOP repudiate Trump's thuggery, or will J.D. Vance consolidate and institutionally entrench it?
The [complicity](https://substack.com/redirect/d58662fa-a9d4-4e16-9fc0-89da4690601c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of so many Democrats in [so many](https://substack.com/redirect/c94d8ab3-a45e-4aee-9bc6-c73fddf3a7b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Trump's actions suggest that the ultimate crux is not the behavior of Republicans, but instead whether voters are able to hold accountable, refashion, replace--or instead remain beholden--to the Democratic Party.

No issue better demonstrates the disturbing pattern of Democrats enabling Trump's authoritarian aspirations than arbitrary state assassinations.
### **Assassinations made for social media**
Last week, observers [across](https://substack.com/redirect/8c0b1ebc-4a2a-4941-ae97-1da232e98e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the [political](https://substack.com/redirect/987cacfd-94e9-4a86-bc53-162299b92d2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) spectrum condemned (while an equally bipartisan spectrum of voices outrageously [supported](https://substack.com/redirect/3b096afd-bfe4-48d5-802d-72e3b93d3848?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) a military strike on a boat in the Caribbean that allegedly was being used by narcotraffickers to transport illegal drugs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/20d2f4c9-08e5-47fd-9bcc-aa0ed30767b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The legal sleights of hand enabling the strike included a recent [reclassification](https://substack.com/redirect/58a2ff6a-3506-4a28-9a34-a94a32b7b4d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Venezuelan gangs as supposed narcoterrorists, as well as a decision (made within a month of Trump retaking the White House) to [ease restrictions](https://substack.com/redirect/24843094-dbec-447d-b2b5-45deb20ec24a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on military airstrikes.
But Kenneth Roth, former executive director of Human Rights Watch, [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/83edd79b-b258-4d4c-a5b6-6015a458f15c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) why these contrived justifications hold little water:
> Under international standards for law enforcement, lethal force can be used solely as a last resort to meet an imminent threat of death or serious bodily injury. That rule makes sense because law-enforcement officials should ordinarily seek to arrest and prosecute criminal suspects. That is the best way to ensure they have committed the offense in question. It also respects the fact that for most crimes, the penalty upon conviction is a prison sentence, not the death penalty--let alone summary killing without trial.
> Trump has sought to evade those standards by in effect declaring war against Venezuelan drug cartels. Beginning with Richard Nixon in 1971, US presidents have repeatedly referred to a "war on drugs", but that was a metaphoric war, a rhetorical claim that the effort was important, not a literal war. The distinction is important, because in genuine armed conflicts, opposing combatants can be summarily shot unless they are surrendering or in custody. There is ordinarily no duty to try to capture or arrest them.
> There was nothing in the encounter in the Caribbean Sea that is indicative of a war.
Adam Isacson, Director for Defense Oversight at the Washington Office on Latin America, [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/bd0120de-a852-4559-95ba-1bc8202760d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "There is zero evidence of self-defense....Looks like a massacre of civilians at sea. Even if they had drugs aboard, that's not a capital offense."
Dan Froomkin, whose bylines have appeared in _The Washington Post_ and _The Intercept_ , [described](https://substack.com/redirect/b7343554-0cc1-4f51-82ca-52e7262e4af9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the strike as a "Horrifying story, and yet horrifyingly understated. This is a five-alarm fire. These are war crimes. This is murder. Trump should go to prison for this. Servicemembers should have refused their orders."
Make no mistake: murdering people through military airstrikes, rather than proving their guilt in a criminal trial, is a gateway to every horror imaginable. This is precisely why the presumption of innocence matters so much. Without it, our government would (and seems to, [indeed](https://substack.com/redirect/b16f530e-681a-41a4-8e66-824b28ad86f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) simply kill people randomly.

But methinks the ranks of [outraged opinion columnists](https://open.substack.com/pub/markbrenton242316/p/when-law-becomes-costume?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios) doth protest too much. Or, more accurately, they're raising their voices a decade late.
### **The complicity of supposed "liberals"**
Presumptive guilt justifying arbitrary state assassination was a power normalized not under a Republican president, but under President Obama, who infamously pioneered arbitrary drone strikes in the War on Terror and even [decided himself](https://substack.com/redirect/aeb52eba-a2f4-401e-8f85-25bdfc4cd03a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who would wind up on the CIA's so-called "kill list."
There are many reasons why so many of us have been screaming bloody murder for [over a decade](https://substack.com/redirect/d974ca11-d8a1-44db-808f-d45278e761bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), while a generation of complacent self-described "liberals" (who couldn't even understand their own supposed commitments) grew complicit, aggrandizing executive power and sleepwalking into today's constitutional crisis.
Both [Obama and Biden helped prepare](https://substack.com/redirect/b931c58b-2608-4464-865b-eb3aeab94444?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the presidency for Trump's autocracy, yet enjoyed unflagging support from the liberal establishment. Similarly, both administrations enjoyed broad support from organized labor despite their [refusal](https://substack.com/redirect/be1f6e8a-17d6-4c54-b16f-e574f0546572?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to support labor's most critical demands.
In each arena, acts of [political theater](https://substack.com/redirect/db40eb5f-02bd-4961-afc4-585cf83c3a36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) were taken in lieu of [actual solidarity](https://substack.com/redirect/be1f6e8a-17d6-4c54-b16f-e574f0546572?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or representation, allowing communities to be [fleeced](https://substack.com/redirect/b4897021-0aef-4ab6-be81-bd2f388f72c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by wolves [masquerading](https://substack.com/redirect/d9aef390-ef39-4018-ad60-cf94314b3949?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as fellow sheep.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3NzMxODEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM2NTE4MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1v7nvDKlgK43gd0-VS28EfpWj0qBaB5UVpMWYkmfk3Q)
I [wrote over a decade ago](https://substack.com/redirect/147c7890-b1dc-45a9-ac30-a4f221fe26ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to expose the lawlessness of the Obama administration's use of drone strikes, which effectively [killed victims at random](https://substack.com/redirect/41078895-c1d2-4997-940a-1477147200f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) despite widely parroted claims that they were somehow targeted. As I explained in 2013:
> While presenting the leaked memo authorizing al-Awlaki's death as limited to that particular target, _[the [New York] Times](https://substack.com/redirect/a39a27ea-76e9-4278-8cce-f75bd81949be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ acknowledges](https://substack.com/redirect/a39a27ea-76e9-4278-8cce-f75bd81949be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that at least two other US citizens--against whom drone strikes were supposedly not authorized--were killed without trial. Yet no one has been held accountable, the program has been only reinforced by [CIA Director John] Brennan's confirmation, and the unaccountable use of targeted assassination continues in secret.
> The question ignored by proponents of drone strikes is the same overlooked by those who defend torture: "How do we know our government has the right person?" The overwhelming proportion of detainees released from Guantanamo, or recurring [revelations of surveillance](https://substack.com/redirect/29d519d7-3be2-49e7-87a2-7be1ea956e44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) targeting non-violent activists, reflect our government's poor track record of assessing guilt.
No demonstration of this pattern offered a more jarring example than the [assassination](https://substack.com/redirect/b7e3bf9a-2b32-473f-8319-e6ad01ba07e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of a [16 year-old born in Denver](https://substack.com/redirect/708c553c-842d-41fe-8464-589b611fc1df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) based on his father's (theoretically constitutionally protected) speech. Like a dystopian legal cherry on a rotten political cake, he was killed even after he & his family [previously sought](https://substack.com/redirect/4e1a1a9c-8c97-46cb-889c-5b6abaa1411f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to vindicate his right to due process in court.
A [once-secret legal memo](https://substack.com/redirect/b534446e-e72a-4835-b6fc-41f1718e6deb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) leaked in 2013 followed a [March 2012 speech](https://substack.com/redirect/f7b9373f-8ad2-4774-847a-d8c51e73b6d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by then Attorney General Eric Holder at Northwestern University Law Center, both aiming to justify what apologists described as a policy allowing "targeted killing" without any judicial--let alone due--process.
A few weeks after AG Holder spoke at Northwestern, I delivered a keynote address at a law symposium a few blocks away at Loyola University Chicago Law School. The [article compiled from my remarks](https://substack.com/redirect/b90bad3e-ff86-49c5-8a1a-4962f1c79c21?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is freely available, but in the interest of making it machine readable, here is a relevant excerpt:
> Just last week, the chief prosecutor of our country spoke down the street from here and defended the president's authority to disregard both [the Fifth and Sixth] amendments at will to essentially order the death of a U.S. citizen without any process. Now there's a secret process that the Attorney General claimed would suffice to guard against any potential liberty interest that we might perceive as threatened here, but I want to invite you to think about a couple of things....from a legal realist perspective.
> I will grant that the Attorney General articulated a series of limiting principles that govern whether killing a U.S. citizen is legal, such as the seniority of a target within a known terror network and the imminence of an attack that might ensue from the target's activities. But what value are limiting principles if there is no forum in which to articulate or contest them? And if there is no transparency to the decision--if this is a process that happens entirely behind closed doors--how can we have any faith that the application of those limiting principles reflects any degree of legitimacy?
> The short answer is: we can't. The canard that we can simply construct a division of process that doesn't involve judicial process, that we can cut out Article III of the constitution because it is inconvenient to the executive branch, is not only foolish and draconian, it is authoritarian.
That was over a decade ago, under an administration led by a [former constitutional law professor](https://substack.com/redirect/b931c58b-2608-4464-865b-eb3aeab94444?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (from another law school just across the same city in which Holder & I spoke a few weeks apart) who has served as the Democratic Party's [template](https://substack.com/redirect/14d36691-d604-481a-a97f-3b02e479b9b4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for [potential successors](https://substack.com/redirect/1fe919c3-28ff-4948-af6d-9ccbca53a912?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since then.
Few appreciate just how much damage President Obama did as a president to the Constitution he studied as an academic.
I could go on here about [executive secrecy](https://substack.com/redirect/fd38e771-4d6c-44a0-9a10-04d593d78771?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or the various crimes it enables, such as [torture with impunity](https://substack.com/redirect/db6f2d05-8738-4a72-8897-b297adeecf3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), or the disturbing [patterns](https://substack.com/redirect/e90210f8-a7c1-45c4-ac1e-87dd90abfe96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) enabled over time by the [ratchet effect](https://substack.com/redirect/ebdbacac-6537-4588-9163-6811929fcd2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but I've [already done](https://substack.com/redirect/159ed409-3fad-4732-b9bc-63eb05bac35b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that [for years](https://substack.com/redirect/a5c986d1-d927-4a03-94d1-61547abb1fb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and will let my [previous](https://substack.com/redirect/be42cfcd-e0fd-4a49-a5ea-9316787ec291?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) writing speak for itself.
### **Can you hear me now?**
It brings me no joy to witness the complicity of the [supposedly liberal opposition](https://substack.com/redirect/7d96ab95-bf4b-465c-a485-41c77e5af524?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the authoritarianism that so many Democrats rightfully decry today.
But it's crucial to witness that complicity in order to enable accountability. Replacing the dominant party in elected offices offers voters no solution to bipartisan issues--and few goals are more compelling than the need to ensure the most basic modicum of due process as the Trump administration continues its [blitzkrieg on America](https://substack.com/redirect/e65f6e69-8799-4252-844f-d230e3961d64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
As we seek to craft an alternative future that might one day finally respect our rights, it's important to recognize the underlying constitutional landscape often obscured by daily partisan debates--and to hold accountable Democrats and Republicans alike for the many authoritarian [policy positions](https://substack.com/redirect/d58662fa-a9d4-4e16-9fc0-89da4690601c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that they have [long shared](https://substack.com/redirect/4f5a4cff-a7d9-4ece-8cb1-8102101ccce0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in common.
_Paid subscribers can get access to some further thoughts reporting on the ground from the front line of the culture war. I found myself intrigued by an object of viral debate and put on my shoes to pound some pavement and investigate the story in person._
### **Manufactured outrage**
I ate last week at a Cracker Barrel for the first time, motivated by the recent--and [foolish](https://substack.com/redirect/191317d0-49ae-4b3c-b469-bc89fe013c68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and ultimately [opportunistic](https://substack.com/redirect/113c01b9-5ff5-4d74-b11f-3686218ac23a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--laregly staged [controversy](https://substack.com/redirect/ea575106-ed64-4f87-b753-a7654034613a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over the company's logo redesign.
The food wasn't bad. I'd describe it as kind of like a cross between Boston Market and Denny's. I ordered fried chicken with gravy, mac & cheese and fried okra, and I have to say it was all really quite good: heavy, rich, and conducive to a post-meal nap.
Even more than by the food, I was most struck by my conversation with the young lady with whom I settled my bill after dinner. I asked whether the logo redesign controversy had affected her at all, and she responded that I was far from the first recent customer to raise the issue.
She said it had generally drawn a great deal of concern. A lot of it seemed unhinged.
She reported being called an insurrectionist by an irate customer, which felt particularly jarring and disjointed given her identity as a Black woman.
She also said that a different customer accused her of _blasphemy_. Because she works at a chain restaurant that changed its logo.
The absurdity felt palpable.
Meanwhile, genocide continues, as well as a domestic war on our rights, our communities, and our Constitution waged by an executive branch subject to increasingly few checks and balances.
The future vector of the United States is clear. Where it leads, no one can be certain.
But I do think it has reached a point at which free thinking people might want to seriously consider getting out while one still can. I would, myself, if I had the financial resources to do so comfortably.

The of so many Democrats in of Trump's actions suggest that the ultimate crux is not the behavior of Republicans, but instead whether voters are able to hold accountable, refashion, replace--or instead remain beholden--to the Democratic Party. No issue better demonstrates the disturbing pattern of Democrats enabling Trump's authoritarian aspirations than arbitrary state assassinations. Assassinations made for social media Last week, observers the spectrum condemned (while an equally bipartisan spectrum of voices outrageously ) a military strike on a boat in the Caribbean that allegedly was being used by narcotraffickers to transport illegal drugs. | | ---|---|--- The legal sleights of hand enabling the strike included a recent of Venezuelan gangs as supposed narcoterrorists, as well as a decision (made within a month of Trump retaking the White House) to on military airstrikes. But Kenneth Roth, former executive director of Human Rights Watch, why these contrived justifications hold little water: Under international standards for law enforcement, lethal force can be used solely as a last resort to meet an imminent threat of death or serious bodily injury. That rule makes sense because law-enforcement officials should ordinarily seek to arrest and prosecute criminal suspects. That is the best way to ensure they have committed the offense in question. It also respects the fact that for most crimes, the penalty upon conviction is a prison sentence, not the death penalty--let alone summary killing without trial. Trump has sought to evade those standards by in effect declaring war against Venezuelan drug cartels. Beginning with Richard Nixon in 1971, US presidents have repeatedly referred to a "war on drugs", but that was a metaphoric war, a rhetorical claim that the effort was important, not a literal war. The distinction is important, because in genuine armed conflicts, opposing combatants can be summarily shot unless they are surrendering or in custody. There is ordinarily no duty to try to capture or arrest them. There was nothing in the encounter in the Caribbean Sea that is indicative of a war. Adam Isacson, Director for Defense Oversight at the Washington Office on Latin America, that "There is zero evidence of self-defense....Looks like a massacre of civilians at sea. Even if they had drugs aboard, that's not a capital offense." Dan Froomkin, whose bylines have appeared in The Washington Post and The Intercept , the strike as a "Horrifying story, and yet horrifyingly understated. This is a five-alarm fire. These are war crimes. This is murder. Trump should go to prison for this. Servicemembers should have refused their orders." Make no mistake: murdering people through military airstrikes, rather than proving their guilt in a criminal trial, is a gateway to every horror imaginable. This is precisely why the presumption of innocence matters so much. Without it, our government would (and seems to, ) simply kill people randomly. But methinks the ranks of doth protest too much. Or, more accurately, they're raising their voices a decade late. The complicity of supposed "liberals" Presumptive guilt justifying arbitrary state assassination was a power normalized not under a Republican president, but under President Obama, who infamously pioneered arbitrary drone strikes in the War on Terror and even who would wind up on the CIA's so-called "kill list." There are many reasons why so many of us have been screaming bloody murder for , while a generation of complacent self-described "liberals" (who couldn't even understand their own supposed commitments) grew complicit, aggrandizing executive power and sleepwalking into today's constitutional crisis. Both the presidency for Trump's autocracy, yet enjoyed unflagging support from the liberal establishment. Similarly, both administrations enjoyed broad support from organized labor despite their to support labor's most critical demands. In each arena, acts of were taken in lieu of or representation, allowing communities to be by wolves as fellow sheep. I to expose the lawlessness of the Obama administration's use of drone strikes, which effectively despite widely parroted claims that they were somehow targeted. As I explained in 2013: While presenting the leaked memo authorizing al-Awlaki's death as limited to that particular target, [the [New York] Times]( that at least two other US citizens--against whom drone strikes were supposedly not authorized--were killed without trial. Yet no one has been held accountable, the program has been only reinforced by [CIA Director John] Brennan's confirmation, and the unaccountable use of targeted assassination continues in secret. The question ignored by proponents of drone strikes is the same overlooked by those who defend torture: "How do we know our government has the right person?" The overwhelming proportion of detainees released from Guantanamo, or recurring targeting non-violent activists, reflect our government's poor track record of assessing guilt. No demonstration of this pattern offered a more jarring example than the of a based on his father's (theoretically constitutionally protected) speech. Like a dystopian legal cherry on a rotten political cake, he was killed even after he & his family to vindicate his right to due process in court. A leaked in 2013 followed a by then Attorney General Eric Holder at Northwestern University Law Center, both aiming to justify what apologists described as a policy allowing "targeted killing" without any judicial--let alone due--process. A few weeks after AG Holder spoke at Northwestern, I delivered a keynote address at a law symposium a few blocks away at Loyola University Chicago Law School. The is freely available, but in the interest of making it machine readable, here is a relevant excerpt: Just last week, the chief prosecutor of our country spoke down the street from here and defended the president's authority to disregard both [the Fifth and Sixth] amendments at will to essentially order the death of a U.S. citizen without any process. Now there's a secret process that the Attorney General claimed would suffice to guard against any potential liberty interest that we might perceive as threatened here, but I want to invite you to think about a couple of things....from a legal realist perspective. I will grant that the Attorney General articulated a series of limiting principles that govern whether killing a U.S. citizen is legal, such as the seniority of a target within a known terror network and the imminence of an attack that might ensue from the target's activities. But what value are limiting principles if there is no forum in which to articulate or contest them? And if there is no transparency to the decision--if this is a process that happens entirely behind closed doors--how can we have any faith that the application of those limiting principles reflects any degree of legitimacy? The short answer is: we can't. The canard that we can simply construct a division of process that doesn't involve judicial process, that we can cut out Article III of the constitution because it is inconvenient to the executive branch, is not only foolish and draconian, it is authoritarian. That was over a decade ago, under an administration led by a (from another law school just across the same city in which Holder & I spoke a few weeks apart) who has served as the Democratic Party's for since then. Few appreciate just how much damage President Obama did as a president to the Constitution he studied as an academic. I could go on here about or the various crimes it enables, such as , or the disturbing enabled over time by the , but I've that and will let my writing speak for itself. Can you hear me now? It brings me no joy to witness the complicity of the in the authoritarianism that so many Democrats rightfully decry today. But it's crucial to witness that complicity in order to enable accountability. Replacing the dominant party in elected offices offers voters no solution to bipartisan issues--and few goals are more compelling than the need to ensure the most basic modicum of due process as the Trump administration continues its . As we seek to craft an alternative future that might one day finally respect our rights, it's important to recognize the underlying constitutional landscape often obscured by daily partisan debates--and to hold accountable Democrats and Republicans alike for the many authoritarian that they have in common. Paid subscribers can get access to some further thoughts reporting on the ground from the front line of the culture war. I found myself intrigued by an object of viral debate and put on my shoes to pound some pavement and investigate the story in person. Manufactured outrage I ate last week at a Cracker Barrel for the first time, motivated by the recent--and , and ultimately staged over the company's logo redesign. The food wasn't bad. I'd describe it as kind of like a cross between Boston Market and Denny's. I ordered fried chicken with gravy, mac & cheese and fried okra, and I have to say it was all really quite good: heavy, rich, and conducive to a post-meal nap. Even more than by the food, I was most struck by my conversation with the young lady with whom I settled my bill after dinner. I asked whether the logo redesign controversy had affected her at all, and she responded that I was far from the first recent customer to raise the issue. She said it had generally drawn a great deal of concern. A lot of it seemed unhinged. She reported being called an insurrectionist by an irate customer, which felt particularly jarring and disjointed given her identity as a Black woman. She also said that a different customer accused her of blasphemy. Because she works at a chain restaurant that changed its logo. The absurdity felt palpable. Meanwhile, genocide continues, as well as a domestic war on our rights, our communities, and our Constitution waged by an executive branch subject to increasingly few checks and balances. The future vector of the United States is clear. Where it leads, no one can be certain. But I do think it has reached a point at which free thinking people might want to seriously consider getting out while one still can. I would, myself, if I had the financial resources to do so comfortably. You're currently a free subscriber to . Among the Trump administration's various daily [assaults on democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/c41928f9-284e-4c4c-ae29-940d34fe4c39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in America, it can be easy to overlook examples that ultimately become inflection points. A close look at what might seem like one-among-many, however, reveals further depths of the emerging dystopia.
Many have wondered what will happen when Trump finally [joins Henry Kissinger in hell](https://substack.com/redirect/e3d1e141-4314-4ba4-afd5-4e397f7b33f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Will the GOP repudiate Trump's thuggery, or will J.D. Vance consolidate and institutionally entrench it?
The [complicity](https://substack.com/redirect/d58662fa-a9d4-4e16-9fc0-89da4690601c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of so many Democrats in [so many](https://substack.com/redirect/c94d8ab3-a45e-4aee-9bc6-c73fddf3a7b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Trump's actions suggest that the ultimate crux is not the behavior of Republicans, but instead whether voters are able to hold accountable, refashion, replace--or instead remain beholden--to the Democratic Party.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTczNzg4NjksImV4cCI6MTc1OTk3MDg2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.fVmGh4ck-SH05lxD7SM7LSyJECqqyTvHnR0okL9Jf7Y)
No issue better demonstrates the disturbing pattern of Democrats enabling Trump's authoritarian aspirations than arbitrary state assassinations.
### **Assassinations made for social media**
Last week, observers [across](https://substack.com/redirect/8c0b1ebc-4a2a-4941-ae97-1da232e98e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the [political](https://substack.com/redirect/987cacfd-94e9-4a86-bc53-162299b92d2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) spectrum condemned (while an equally bipartisan spectrum of voices outrageously [supported](https://substack.com/redirect/3b096afd-bfe4-48d5-802d-72e3b93d3848?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) a military strike on a boat in the Caribbean that allegedly was being used by narcotraffickers to transport illegal drugs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/20d2f4c9-08e5-47fd-9bcc-aa0ed30767b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The legal sleights of hand enabling the strike included a recent [reclassification](https://substack.com/redirect/58a2ff6a-3506-4a28-9a34-a94a32b7b4d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Venezuelan gangs as supposed narcoterrorists, as well as a decision (made within a month of Trump retaking the White House) to [ease restrictions](https://substack.com/redirect/24843094-dbec-447d-b2b5-45deb20ec24a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on military airstrikes.
But Kenneth Roth, former executive director of Human Rights Watch, [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/83edd79b-b258-4d4c-a5b6-6015a458f15c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) why these contrived justifications hold little water:
> Under international standards for law enforcement, lethal force can be used solely as a last resort to meet an imminent threat of death or serious bodily injury. That rule makes sense because law-enforcement officials should ordinarily seek to arrest and prosecute criminal suspects. That is the best way to ensure they have committed the offense in question. It also respects the fact that for most crimes, the penalty upon conviction is a prison sentence, not the death penalty--let alone summary killing without trial.
> Trump has sought to evade those standards by in effect declaring war against Venezuelan drug cartels. Beginning with Richard Nixon in 1971, US presidents have repeatedly referred to a "war on drugs", but that was a metaphoric war, a rhetorical claim that the effort was important, not a literal war. The distinction is important, because in genuine armed conflicts, opposing combatants can be summarily shot unless they are surrendering or in custody. There is ordinarily no duty to try to capture or arrest them.
> There was nothing in the encounter in the Caribbean Sea that is indicative of a war.
Adam Isacson, Director for Defense Oversight at the Washington Office on Latin America, [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/bd0120de-a852-4559-95ba-1bc8202760d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "There is zero evidence of self-defense....Looks like a massacre of civilians at sea. Even if they had drugs aboard, that's not a capital offense."
Dan Froomkin, whose bylines have appeared in _The Washington Post_ and _The Intercept_ , [described](https://substack.com/redirect/b7343554-0cc1-4f51-82ca-52e7262e4af9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the strike as a "Horrifying story, and yet horrifyingly understated. This is a five-alarm fire. These are war crimes. This is murder. Trump should go to prison for this. Servicemembers should have refused their orders."
Make no mistake: murdering people through military airstrikes, rather than proving their guilt in a criminal trial, is a gateway to every horror imaginable. This is precisely why the presumption of innocence matters so much. Without it, our government would (and seems to, [indeed](https://substack.com/redirect/b16f530e-681a-41a4-8e66-824b28ad86f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) simply kill people randomly.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..gkRvHh2nTkqXNddYwu94-RsGEN84EWOhvUBBEY1VQqc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173143355)
But methinks the ranks of [outraged opinion columnists](https://open.substack.com/pub/markbrenton242316/p/when-law-becomes-costume?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios) doth protest too much. Or, more accurately, they're raising their voices a decade late.
### **The complicity of supposed "liberals"**
Presumptive guilt justifying arbitrary state assassination was a power normalized not under a Republican president, but under President Obama, who infamously pioneered arbitrary drone strikes in the War on Terror and even [decided himself](https://substack.com/redirect/aeb52eba-a2f4-401e-8f85-25bdfc4cd03a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who would wind up on the CIA's so-called "kill list."
There are many reasons why so many of us have been screaming bloody murder for [over a decade](https://substack.com/redirect/d974ca11-d8a1-44db-808f-d45278e761bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), while a generation of complacent self-described "liberals" (who couldn't even understand their own supposed commitments) grew complicit, aggrandizing executive power and sleepwalking into today's constitutional crisis.
Both [Obama and Biden helped prepare](https://substack.com/redirect/b931c58b-2608-4464-865b-eb3aeab94444?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the presidency for Trump's autocracy, yet enjoyed unflagging support from the liberal establishment. Similarly, both administrations enjoyed broad support from organized labor despite their [refusal](https://substack.com/redirect/be1f6e8a-17d6-4c54-b16f-e574f0546572?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to support labor's most critical demands.
In each arena, acts of [political theater](https://substack.com/redirect/db40eb5f-02bd-4961-afc4-585cf83c3a36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) were taken in lieu of [actual solidarity](https://substack.com/redirect/be1f6e8a-17d6-4c54-b16f-e574f0546572?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or representation, allowing communities to be [fleeced](https://substack.com/redirect/b4897021-0aef-4ab6-be81-bd2f388f72c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by wolves [masquerading](https://substack.com/redirect/d9aef390-ef39-4018-ad60-cf94314b3949?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as fellow sheep.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173143355&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTQzMzU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTczNzg4NjksImV4cCI6MTc1OTk3MDg2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.fVmGh4ck-SH05lxD7SM7LSyJECqqyTvHnR0okL9Jf7Y)
I [wrote over a decade ago](https://substack.com/redirect/147c7890-b1dc-45a9-ac30-a4f221fe26ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to expose the lawlessness of the Obama administration's use of drone strikes, which effectively [killed victims at random](https://substack.com/redirect/41078895-c1d2-4997-940a-1477147200f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) despite widely parroted claims that they were somehow targeted. As I explained in 2013:
> While presenting the leaked memo authorizing al-Awlaki's death as limited to that particular target, _[the [New York] Times](https://substack.com/redirect/a39a27ea-76e9-4278-8cce-f75bd81949be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ acknowledges](https://substack.com/redirect/a39a27ea-76e9-4278-8cce-f75bd81949be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that at least two other US citizens--against whom drone strikes were supposedly not authorized--were killed without trial. Yet no one has been held accountable, the program has been only reinforced by [CIA Director John] Brennan's confirmation, and the unaccountable use of targeted assassination continues in secret.
> The question ignored by proponents of drone strikes is the same overlooked by those who defend torture: "How do we know our government has the right person?" The overwhelming proportion of detainees released from Guantanamo, or recurring [revelations of surveillance](https://substack.com/redirect/29d519d7-3be2-49e7-87a2-7be1ea956e44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) targeting non-violent activists, reflect our government's poor track record of assessing guilt.
No demonstration of this pattern offered a more jarring example than the [assassination](https://substack.com/redirect/b7e3bf9a-2b32-473f-8319-e6ad01ba07e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of a [16 year-old born in Denver](https://substack.com/redirect/708c553c-842d-41fe-8464-589b611fc1df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) based on his father's (theoretically constitutionally protected) speech. Like a dystopian legal cherry on a rotten political cake, he was killed even after he & his family [previously sought](https://substack.com/redirect/4e1a1a9c-8c97-46cb-889c-5b6abaa1411f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to vindicate his right to due process in court.
A [once-secret legal memo](https://substack.com/redirect/b534446e-e72a-4835-b6fc-41f1718e6deb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) leaked in 2013 followed a [March 2012 speech](https://substack.com/redirect/f7b9373f-8ad2-4774-847a-d8c51e73b6d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by then Attorney General Eric Holder at Northwestern University Law Center, both aiming to justify what apologists described as a policy allowing "targeted killing" without any judicial--let alone due--process.
A few weeks after AG Holder spoke at Northwestern, I delivered a keynote address at a law symposium a few blocks away at Loyola University Chicago Law School. The [article compiled from my remarks](https://substack.com/redirect/b90bad3e-ff86-49c5-8a1a-4962f1c79c21?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is freely available, but in the interest of making it machine readable, here is a relevant excerpt:
> Just last week, the chief prosecutor of our country spoke down the street from here and defended the president's authority to disregard both [the Fifth and Sixth] amendments at will to essentially order the death of a U.S. citizen without any process. Now there's a secret process that the Attorney General claimed would suffice to guard against any potential liberty interest that we might perceive as threatened here, but I want to invite you to think about a couple of things....from a legal realist perspective.
> I will grant that the Attorney General articulated a series of limiting principles that govern whether killing a U.S. citizen is legal, such as the seniority of a target within a known terror network and the imminence of an attack that might ensue from the target's activities. But what value are limiting principles if there is no forum in which to articulate or contest them? And if there is no transparency to the decision--if this is a process that happens entirely behind closed doors--how can we have any faith that the application of those limiting principles reflects any degree of legitimacy?
> The short answer is: we can't. The canard that we can simply construct a division of process that doesn't involve judicial process, that we can cut out Article III of the constitution because it is inconvenient to the executive branch, is not only foolish and draconian, it is authoritarian.
That was over a decade ago, under an administration led by a [former constitutional law professor](https://substack.com/redirect/b931c58b-2608-4464-865b-eb3aeab94444?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (from another law school just across the same city in which Holder & I spoke a few weeks apart) who has served as the Democratic Party's [template](https://substack.com/redirect/14d36691-d604-481a-a97f-3b02e479b9b4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for [potential successors](https://substack.com/redirect/1fe919c3-28ff-4948-af6d-9ccbca53a912?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since then.
Few appreciate just how much damage President Obama did as a president to the Constitution he studied as an academic.
I could go on here about [executive secrecy](https://substack.com/redirect/fd38e771-4d6c-44a0-9a10-04d593d78771?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or the various crimes it enables, such as [torture with impunity](https://substack.com/redirect/db6f2d05-8738-4a72-8897-b297adeecf3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), or the disturbing [patterns](https://substack.com/redirect/e90210f8-a7c1-45c4-ac1e-87dd90abfe96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) enabled over time by the [ratchet effect](https://substack.com/redirect/ebdbacac-6537-4588-9163-6811929fcd2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but I've [already done](https://substack.com/redirect/159ed409-3fad-4732-b9bc-63eb05bac35b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that [for years](https://substack.com/redirect/a5c986d1-d927-4a03-94d1-61547abb1fb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and will let my [previous](https://substack.com/redirect/be42cfcd-e0fd-4a49-a5ea-9316787ec291?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) writing speak for itself.
### **Can you hear me now?**
It brings me no joy to witness the complicity of the [supposedly liberal opposition](https://substack.com/redirect/7d96ab95-bf4b-465c-a485-41c77e5af524?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the authoritarianism that so many Democrats rightfully decry today.
But it's crucial to witness that complicity in order to enable accountability. Replacing the dominant party in elected offices offers voters no solution to bipartisan issues--and few goals are more compelling than the need to ensure the most basic modicum of due process as the Trump administration continues its [blitzkrieg on America](https://substack.com/redirect/e65f6e69-8799-4252-844f-d230e3961d64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
As we seek to craft an alternative future that might one day finally respect our rights, it's important to recognize the underlying constitutional landscape often obscured by daily partisan debates--and to hold accountable Democrats and Republicans alike for the many authoritarian [policy positions](https://substack.com/redirect/d58662fa-a9d4-4e16-9fc0-89da4690601c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that they have [long shared](https://substack.com/redirect/4f5a4cff-a7d9-4ece-8cb1-8102101ccce0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in common.
_Paid subscribers can get access to some further thoughts reporting on the ground from the front line of the culture war. I found myself intrigued by an object of viral debate and put on my shoes to pound some pavement and investigate the story in person..._

But Kenneth Roth, former executive director of Human Rights Watch, why these contrived justifications hold little water: Under international standards for law enforcement, lethal force can be used solely as a last resort to meet an imminent threat of death or serious bodily injury. That rule makes sense because law-enforcement officials should ordinarily seek to arrest and prosecute criminal suspects. That is the best way to ensure they have committed the offense in question. It also respects the fact that for most crimes, the penalty upon conviction is a prison sentence, not the death penalty--let alone summary killing without trial. Trump has sought to evade those standards by in effect declaring war against Venezuelan drug cartels. Beginning with Richard Nixon in 1971, US presidents have repeatedly referred to a "war on drugs", but that was a metaphoric war, a rhetorical claim that the effort was important, not a literal war. The distinction is important, because in genuine armed conflicts, opposing combatants can be summarily shot unless they are surrendering or in custody. There is ordinarily no duty to try to capture or arrest them. There was nothing in the encounter in the Caribbean Sea that is indicative of a war. Adam Isacson, Director for Defense Oversight at the Washington Office on Latin America, that "There is zero evidence of self-defense....Looks like a massacre of civilians at sea. Even if they had drugs aboard, that's not a capital offense." These are war crimes. This is murder. Trump should go to prison for this. Servicemembers should have refused their orders." This is precisely why the presumption of innocence matters so much. Without it, our government would (and seems to, ) simply kill people randomly. * * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" NY attorney general will intervene in Texas abortion pill access lawsuit," **Texas Tribune's **Jessica Priest** \-- "New York Attorney General **Letitia James** on Monday said she will intervene in a legal fight over Texas' ban on abortion pills, escalating a national showdown between states that have restricted abortion access and others that are defending practitioners who offer services to out-of-state women.

Her decision came over a month after Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** sued a New York county clerk for refusing to file a six-figure judgment against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion drugs to a Texas woman. This continues a legal battle over shield laws that [experts say will likely](https://substack.com/redirect/c499af84-c796-4d2e-8676-392f8fabad20?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reach the U.S. Supreme Court.
[BECOME A PAID SSUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/d2e5629f-dfdb-4b89-b7e4-703f7a80ae97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I am stepping in to defend the integrity of our laws and our courts against this blatant overreach," James said in a news release. "Texas has no authority in New York, and no power to impose its cruel abortion ban here."

In a statement to The Texas Tribune, Paxton called the New York attorney general "a lawless abortionist" and said that he will defeat her in court.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/d2e5629f-dfdb-4b89-b7e4-703f7a80ae97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The legal fight first started in December 2024 when Paxton [sued](https://substack.com/redirect/2e575d5a-1b1d-4b73-b155-09684056935b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Dr. **Margaret Carpenter** , accusing her of mailing abortion pills from New York to a woman in Collin County in violation of Texas law. Carpenter is the co-founder of the Abortion Coalition for Telemedicine, which helps abortion providers in states with so-called shield laws.

Shield law protections, which exist in [over 20 states](https://substack.com/redirect/99a7a821-9940-4003-938e-b48b4e61a10b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), seek to legally protect health care professionals and others who provide abortion-related services to those who live in states with abortion bans.

In February, a Collin County judge [ordered](https://substack.com/redirect/dd3d69b9-95b8-41a4-9757-7d35de4c9049?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the New York doctor to stop providing abortion pills to Texans and to pay more than $113,000 in penalties and fees.

Acting Ulster County Clerk **Taylor Bruck**[ refused](https://substack.com/redirect/ee6f0267-7769-4d41-b7f9-cc261a5383c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to file the judgment in March and cited his state's shield law, which was passed in 2023 following the overturn of Roe v. Wade.

Bruck again rejected [a second Texas attempt](https://substack.com/redirect/71c6a7a0-af23-443a-938f-abba75136f9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to enforce the judgment in July.

Paxton then sued the clerk [later that month](https://substack.com/redirect/6a66f9ff-fd44-4563-bf6a-c86e9c78881a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Ulster County. His office also argued in a [court document](https://substack.com/redirect/83d6d6af-3cb6-4f4a-846f-0935efbfc7f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that New York's shield law violates the U.S. Constitution's [full faith and credit clause](https://substack.com/redirect/97736db5-3702-4ba9-bcdc-ad6a0b4563b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which requires states to respect other states' "public acts, records, and judicial proceedings."

"No matter where [Carpenter] hides, our pro-life laws will be enforced, and justice will be served," Paxton said Monday. "The Constitution expressly requires states to recognize the judicial enactments of other states' courts, and New York, unsurprisingly, is choosing to ignore that constitutional requirement."

James said she will submit a legal filing later this month to argue that New York has the legal right to safeguard its residents and courts against "out-of-state overreach." She said Monday that she has formally notified the case's judge of that decision.

"Our shield law exists to protect New Yorkers from out-of-state extremists, and New York will always stand strong as a safe haven for health care and freedom of choice," she said in the news release." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/72725e77-4301-47cc-b6fa-2225cfa5da88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS " NY attorney general will intervene in Texas abortion pill access lawsuit," Texas Tribune's Jessica Priest \-- "New York Attorney General Letitia James on Monday said she will intervene in a legal fight over Texas' ban on abortion pills, escalating a national showdown between states that have restricted abortion access and others that are defending practitioners who offer services to out-of-state women. Her decision came over a month after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued a New York county clerk for refusing to file a six-figure judgment against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion drugs to a Texas woman. This continues a legal battle over shield laws that reach the U.S. Supreme Court. "I am stepping in to defend the integrity of our laws and our courts against this blatant overreach," James said in a news release. "Texas has no authority in New York, and no power to impose its cruel abortion ban here." In a statement to The Texas Tribune, Paxton called the New York attorney general "a lawless abortionist" and said that he will defeat her in court. The legal fight first started in December 2024 when Paxton Dr. Margaret Carpenter , accusing her of mailing abortion pills from New York to a woman in Collin County in violation of Texas law. Carpenter is the co-founder of the Abortion Coalition for Telemedicine, which helps abortion providers in states with so-called shield laws. Shield law protections, which exist in , seek to legally protect health care professionals and others who provide abortion-related services to those who live in states with abortion bans. In February, a Collin County judge the New York doctor to stop providing abortion pills to Texans and to pay more than $113,000 in penalties and fees. Acting Ulster County Clerk Taylor Bruck to file the judgment in March and cited his state's shield law, which was passed in 2023 following the overturn of Roe v. Wade. Bruck again rejected to enforce the judgment in July. Paxton then sued the clerk in Ulster County. His office also argued in a that New York's shield law violates the U.S. Constitution's , which requires states to respect other states' "public acts, records, and judicial proceedings." "No matter where [Carpenter] hides, our pro-life laws will be enforced, and justice will be served," Paxton said Monday. "The Constitution expressly requires states to recognize the judicial enactments of other states' courts, and New York, unsurprisingly, is choosing to ignore that constitutional requirement." James said she will submit a legal filing later this month to argue that New York has the legal right to safeguard its residents and courts against "out-of-state overreach." She said Monday that she has formally notified the case's judge of that decision. "Our shield law exists to protect New Yorkers from out-of-state extremists, and New York will always stand strong as a safe haven for health care and freedom of choice," she said in the news release." FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. # MAKE AND MEET IN YOUR COMMUNITY
# Hello friends,
Looking for more ways to support strong leadership in Indianapolis? Want to craft and connect with community members?

Please join me at a **make and meet at Tuggle's Gifts & Goods on Monday, 9/15 from 6 to 7:30 pm**, for an evening of civic creativity. We will decorate tote bags with patches, pins, stencils and more-- all while enjoying bubbly drinks, sweet treats, and great conversation. You'll also get 10% off all shopping in Tuggles while you attend the event. [Get tickets here](https://andreahunley.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5c2b34b98b419cb5ca0511f25&id=91f0c16633&e=d9ebccf591).

Looking forward to getting creative with patches and policy, Andrea
